case_metadata.user_request: Find the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for the adsorbate with SMILES O=N on the provided surface.

plan.reasoning: No prior attempts exist, so I must choose the best physically sensible initial plan. The adsorbate SMILES O=N corresponds to nitric oxide written as a neutral closed-shell two-atom molecule in the provided atom list: O index 0 and N index 1, both SP2, both with radical_electrons = 0. Therefore this must be treated as a Molecule, not a ReactiveSpecies, and any binding index selected must come from these exact indices. For NO-like diatomics on transition-metal-containing surfaces, adsorption is commonly strongest through the N end because N is the better donor to electropositive/transition-metal surface sites, while O-end binding is usually less favorable. On this mixed Ti/In/N surface, Ti is the most plausible strongly interacting adsorption center among In, N, and Ti. Since there is no history and only one attempt is allowed, the most likely low-energy starting point is N-end atop Ti. This satisfies the site alignment rule for ontop with exactly one adsorbate binding index. I therefore choose molecular adsorption via N atom index 1 on an ontop Ti site.
